mnemonics.title: Data Units Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Big Elephants Have Very Big Ears Using Extraordinary Yawns - Bit, Exabyte, HB, VB, EB, UB, ZB, YB (ascending)

mnemonics.title: Memory Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: K-MG-T-P-E-Z-Y - Keep Making Good Toast Properly Every Zealous Year - KB, MB, GB, TB, PB, EB, ZB, YB (each 1024×)

mnemonics.title: Throughput vs Latency
mnemonics.content: Throughput = How MUCH (volume), Latency = How FAST (delay). T-H-R-O-U-G-H = volUMe, L-A-T-E-N-C-Y = dELAY.

acronyms.expansion: Millions of Floating Point Operations Per Second

acronyms.usage: Network traffic prioritization for capacity management
System reliability metric for capacity planning

visualTips.visualization: Think of a building: bits = bricks, bytes = rooms, KB = floors, MB = buildings, GB = city blocks. Each step up is 1024× larger (like 1024 bricks per room).
Highways: Throughput = number of cars per hour (capacity), Latency = time to travel from A to B (delay). 8-lane highway has high throughput but long distance = high latency.
Two cars at same speed (GHz): Car A (efficient engine) delivers more work per mile than Car B. MHz/GHz is speed, actual work = speed × efficiency.

analogies.analogy: Bandwidth is like pipe diameter. A 4-inch pipe moves more water per second than a 1-inch pipe, but water still takes time to travel (latency).
IOPS is like checking out books at a library. You can check out many books at once (high throughput) but each checkout takes time (IOPS matters for transaction speed).
RAM access is like retrieving a book from your desk (instant). Disk access is like requesting a book from another library (takes days). Memory is fast but expensive, disk is slow but cheap.
Traffic jam: Cars waiting at a signal = queue. More lanes (capacity) help, but if signal is slow (high latency), traffic backs up. CPU queue depth affects performance.
